raw_arxiv_papers.title: Characterization of JWST NIRCam PSFs and Implications for AGN+Host Image Decomposition
raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: We present a detailed analysis of the point spread function (PSF) of JWST NIRCam imaging in eight filters: F070W, F115W, F150W, F200W, F277W, F356W, F444W, and F480M, using publicly available data. Spatial variations in the PSF FWHM generally decrease with wavelength: the maximum and RMS fractional variations are $\sim20\%$ and $5\%$ in F070W, reduced to $\sim3\%$ and $0.6\%$ in F444W. We compare three commonly-used methods (SWarp, photutils, and PSFEx) to construct model PSFs and conclude that PSFEx delivers the best performance. Using simulated images of broad-line AGNs, we evaluate the impact of PSF mismatches on the recoverability of host galaxy properties. The background images are created using carefully vetted science and calibration exposures and improve the quality of previous background reference files. We present our methodology to create the background images and assess their quality using background subtracted science data. Overall, background residuals reach below 1% of the sky brightness for all filter and grism combinations, corresponding to a decrease in the background RMS of a factor of up to 7 from previous background reference files. raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: The Zone of Avoidance (ZoA) remains one of the last frontiers in constructing a comprehensive three-dimensional map of the Universe. import os
import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from astropy.io import fits

# Define the data directory
DATA_DIR = os.getenv('DATA_DIR', './data')

# Load the FITS file
fits_file_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'cutout_r.fits')

# Function to compute metrics from the FITS file
def compute_metrics(fits_file_path):
    with fits.open(fits_file_path) as hdul:
        data = hdul[0].data

    # Assuming data is a 2D array of pixel values
    if data is None:
        print("No data found in the FITS file.")
        return

    # Calculate metrics
    peak_magnitude = -2.5 * np.log10(np.max(data))  # Peak magnitude
    amplitude = np.max(data) - np.min(data)  # Amplitude
    half_max = np.where(data >= np.max(data) / 2)[0]  # Time above half-maximum
    time_above_half_max = len(half_max)  # Count of pixels above half-maximum

    # Print metrics
    print(f"PEAK_MAGNITUDE: {peak_magnitude:.2f}")
    print(f"AMPLITUDE: {amplitude:.2f}")
    print(f"TIME_ABOVE_HALF_MAX: {time_above_half_max}")

# Execute the metric computation
compute_metrics(fits_file_path)

# Print agreement on code use
supervisor_needs_code = False
aggregator_needs_code = True
agreement = supervisor_needs_code == aggregator_needs_code
print(f"AGREEMENT_ON_CODE_USE: {agreement}")

raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: We present a detailed analysis of the point spread function (PSF) of JWST NIRCam imaging in eight filters: F070W, F115W, F150W, F200W, F277W, F356W, F444W, and F480M, using publicly available data.
